content.problemStatement:
Sereja has an undirected graph consisting of NNN vertices and MMM edges. Sereja is interested in one question: is it possible to delete edges from the graph in a way such that the degree of each vertex in the final graph is exactly 111?

The degree of any vertex in the graph is defined as the number of edges connected to that vertex.

Please help Sereja.
constraints: There can be multiple edges but no self loops in the graph.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains a single integer -  TTT, the number of test cases. TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two space separated integers - NNN and MMM. The description of the edges follows.
The next MMM lines contain two space separated integers — UUU and VVV - representing an edge connecting vertex UUU and vertex VVV.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print YES if it is possible, otherwise print NO.
